title: Sched: Conditionally fire VIF triggers only on value change

features.name: virtual interface assignment to interface instance
features.description: Code should include a virtual interface variable assigned to a concrete interface instance

features.name: combinational logic assign to virtual interface member
features.description: Code should include a continuous assign statement that writes to a virtual interface member signal, creating a pattern where the same value may be written repeatedly

features.name: class task writing to virtual interface member
features.description: Code should include a class method that writes through a virtual interface handle to an interface member, which can cause convergence issues when called repeatedly with same value
features.snippet:
interface INTF(); logic [7:0] data; endinterface
class Writer; virtual INTF vif; task write_data(logic [7:0] d); vif.data = d; endtask endclass

features.name: multiple virtual interface instances same type
features.description: Code should include multiple virtual interface handles referencing different instances of the same interface type, where writes to one instance can trigger spurious re-evaluation of the other

features.name: always_ff with vif member assignment in class context
features.description: Code should include an always_ff block that assigns to a virtual interface member through a class field, a pattern that can cause NBA region convergence issues
features.snippet: class Driver; virtual interface IFace vif; always_ff @(posedge vif.clk) vif.data <= '0; endclass
